snapshot.one_liner: Engineers and applied ML practitioners shipping AI-powered product features who need behavioral data to understand whether their AI is actually working.
archetypes: ML/AI engineer embedding LLMs or models into product surfaces
Full-stack engineer owning an AI feature end-to-end
AI product lead with hands-on technical depth

items: Ship AI features that demonstrably improve user outcomes — not just technically functional, but behaviorally validated
Understand how users actually interact with AI outputs (accept, ignore, edit, abandon)
Iterate on model behavior based on real usage signals, not just offline evals
Justify AI investment to product and leadership with usage and impact data
Reduce time-to-insight between model change and measurable user behavior shift
Can't connect model performance metrics (latency, accuracy) to downstream user behavior — two separate systems
No standard way to measure whether AI suggestions are accepted, edited, or ignored
Feature flags and experiments don't account for AI-specific patterns (e.g., prompt variation, model version)
Instrumentation is manual and bespoke — every team builds their own event schema for AI interactions
Slow feedback loop: model ships, behavior changes, but nobody knows why retention moved
Hard to distinguish 'users don't like the AI' from 'users don't understand the AI exists'
Speed to instrument and query — they won't use a tool that adds overhead to their iteration cycle
Ability to define custom events for AI-specific interactions (suggestion shown, accepted, regenerated, etc.)
Experiment support that handles model A/B tests, not just UI changes
Team-level adoption: if PM and data science can self-serve, that's a win
SDK quality and docs — they will read the source code
Integrates with their existing stack (don't want another data silo)

items.objection: We already have logging / internal dashboards
items.response: Logs tell you what happened. Amplitude tells you why users behaved the way they did — and connects AI feature usage to retention, activation, and monetization outcomes.
Those tools weren't built for AI feature analysis. Amplitude's behavioral graph and experiment layer are purpose-fit for understanding multi-turn AI interactions at scale.
Amplitude is built so engineers can instrument and query without bottlenecking on data team cycles — and data teams get richer signal without building pipelines from scratch.
The cost of shipping AI features blind — iterating on gut instead of signal — is higher. Ask what one wasted model sprint costs vs. a quarter of Amplitude.

messaging.headline: See exactly how users respond to your AI — and ship the next version faster
messaging.subheader: Amplitude gives AI builders the behavioral layer to instrument AI interactions, run experiments on model changes, and connect AI feature usage to product outcomes.

value_pillars.pillar: Instrument AI interactions, not just pageviews
value_pillars.detail: Track suggestion shown, accepted, edited, dismissed — and build a behavioral model of how your AI is actually used.

value_pillars.pillar: Experiment on model behavior, not just UI
value_pillars.detail: Run A/B tests on prompt variations, model versions, or feature rollouts — and see behavioral impact in hours, not weeks.

value_pillars.pillar: Connect AI usage to business outcomes
value_pillars.detail: Know which AI interactions drive retention, activation, and expansion — so you can prioritize the right model improvements.

proof_preferences: Code samples and SDK docs they can evaluate themselves
Case studies from AI-native companies (not traditional SaaS)
Concrete metric examples: 'teams using Amplitude reduced AI feature iteration cycles by X%'
Live demo with their own event schema, not a canned walkthrough

how_they_talk: Direct, technical, skeptical of marketing language
Respect for specificity — vague claims get tuned out immediately
Peer-to-peer: they respond to engineers talking to engineers
Show don't tell: demos and docs beat decks
phrases_that_resonate: Close the loop between model change and user behavior
Stop flying blind on AI feature performance
phrases_to_avoid: AI-powered analytics (when describing Amplitude — they want analytics for AI, not analytics with AI)

buying_motion.pattern: Bottom-up: AI Builder instruments a project, gets value, advocates internally. PM and data team get pulled in. Lands as a team or org purchase.
buying_motion.champion_profile: The engineer who shipped the AI feature and got asked 'is this actually working?' by their PM or CTO — and had no clean answer
buying_motion.expansion_trigger: When AI features ship to more surfaces or teams — each new feature needs its own behavioral instrumentation
buying_motion.time_to_value: Should feel fast: instrument in a day, first insights same week. Anything slower breaks the loop.

amplitude_differentiation.versus_generic_analytics: Built for behavioral depth, not just event counts — AI builders need funnels, retention curves, and path analysis on AI interactions, not dashboards
amplitude_differentiation.versus_internal_tools: Amplitude ships in days, scales to billions of events, and gives PM + data science self-serve access without engineering support
amplitude_differentiation.versus_model_observability_tools: Those tools see the model. Amplitude sees the user. You need both — but user behavior is the ultimate measure of whether AI is working.
